stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (solar panels) yields a guaranteed return of $133K. Option B (wind turbines) depends on weather patterns: with probability 37-59% (uncertain), it yields $203K; otherwise it yields $60K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in weather patterns affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $133K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $112.9K to $144.4K. Point estimate: $128.6K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 51.0%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 51.0%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
